pattern_recognition: String Manipulation, Two Pointer Technique. The problem requires recognizing patterns in strings and validating their properties under specific conditions (ignoring non-alphanumeric characters and case). Signals include the need for efficient traversal and comparison of characters.

approaches.title: Brute Force
approaches.intuition: Check if the string is equal to its reverse after filtering out non-alphanumeric characters.
approaches.algorithm: 1. Filter the string to keep only alphanumeric characters and convert to lower case. 2. Reverse the filtered string. 3. Compare the filtered string with its reverse.

approaches.title: Optimal Approach
approaches.intuition: Use a two-pointer technique to compare characters from both ends of the filtered string, moving towards the center.
approaches.algorithm: 1. Initialize two pointers at the start and end of the filtered string. 2. Compare characters at both pointers, moving inward. 3. If characters do not match, return false. 4. If pointers cross, return true.

dry_run: Input: 'A man, a plan, a canal: Panama'. After filtering, we get 'amanaplanacanalpanama'. Using the optimal approach, we set pointers at 'a' (start) and 'a' (end). They match, move inward to 'm' and 'm', continue until pointers cross, confirming it's a palindrome.
edge_cases: Empty input should return true. A single character input should return true. Duplicates like 'aa' should return true. Large inputs should be handled efficiently without performance issues. Non-alphanumeric characters should be ignored.
common_pitfalls: 1. Forgetting to ignore non-alphanumeric characters can lead to incorrect results. 2. Not handling case sensitivity can cause false negatives. 3. Using inefficient methods that do not scale well with larger strings can lead to performance issues.
